extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 3–4 time steps, values start relatively high (around 0.2–0.3) and then clearly decrease.
R2: Between time steps 4–7, values reach a low plateau close to zero (roughly below 0.05).
R3: From about time 8 to 13, values increase steeply from near zero to a peak around 0.6–0.7.
R4: After the main peak (around time 13), values decrease, but a secondary bump/shoulder (local maximum) still appears before the final descent.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 3–4 time steps, values stay low and near-flat, close to zero (roughly below 0.05) without an initial high point.
R2: From time 4 to about 9, values remain low and only slowly increase, still below about 0.15 until near time 9.
R3: From about time 9 to 14, values rise sharply from low to a peak around 0.6–0.7, similar in height and timing to class 0.
R4: After the main peak, values decline smoothly without a pronounced secondary bump/shoulder; the descent towards the end is more monotonic.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 3–4 time steps, the series starts at a moderate value (around 0.2–0.3) and clearly decreases toward near 0.  
R2: From roughly time 4–6 until 11–12, the series stays low (close to 0) before a strong rise.  
R3: Around time 12–14, the series has a single main peak near 0.6–0.7, then decreases.  
R4: After the peak, the series steadily falls toward the end, reaching values below about 0.3.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 3–4 time steps, the series starts very low (near 0) and remains almost flat and close to 0.  
R2: From roughly time 4–10, the series gradually increases from near 0 to moderate values, without an early moderate starting level.  
R3: Between about time 10–18, the series shows one or two high peaks around 0.6–0.7.  
R4: After the last peak, the series decreases toward the end, but starts that descent from a higher level than in Class 0 (the rise begins later and from closer to 0).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts at a moderately high value (around 0.2–0.3) and then decreases during the first 5–7 time steps to near zero.  
R2: From this early low point, it rises steadily and strongly from about time 7–8 to reach a main peak (around 0.6–0.7) near time 12–13.  
R3: After this main peak, it shows a plateau or secondary bump around time 15–18, staying fairly high (around 0.5–0.7).  
R4: From about time 18 onward, it decreases steadily to a mid‑low value (around 0.15–0.3) by the end, but does not return as close to zero as the early minimum.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a low value (near 0) and remains very low and flat for the first 5–7 time steps.  
R2: From this extended low region, it then rises strongly, first to an intermediate peak around time 11–13 (about 0.5–0.6).  
R3: After a small dip or plateau, it continues to rise to a later, main peak (around 0.6–0.7) occurring nearer time 16–18 than the first peak.  
R4: Following this late main peak, it declines steadily toward a low value (around 0.1–0.2) by the end, roughly returning close to its initial scale.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts from a moderate value (around 0.2–0.3) and decreases steadily to near zero in the early part (roughly first quarter of the length).  
R2: After this early dip, the series rises sharply from near zero to a single clear main peak around the middle (value about 0.6–0.7).  
R3: Following the main peak, the series gently oscillates at a relatively high level (around 0.5–0.7) rather than dropping immediately.  
R4: Only after this high plateau/oscillation does the series fall back down toward low values near the end.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts from a very low value (near zero) and remains almost flat and close to zero for a noticeable initial segment.  
R2: From this flat low start, the series then climbs smoothly and continuously toward a high region, without an initial decrease.  
R3: Around the middle, there is a pronounced main peak (about 0.6–0.7), often preceded by a smaller “shoulder” peak.  
R4: After the main peak, the series descends more directly and steadily back toward low values, without a prolonged high plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 2–3 time steps, values start around 0.2–0.3 and clearly decrease toward near 0.  
R2: From about time 3–7, the series stays very low and flat, close to 0.  
R3: From about time 8–13, there is a strong, almost monotonic rise from near 0 up to around 0.6–0.7.  
R4: After the main peak (around time 13–15), values remain relatively high (>0.3) for several steps before declining.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 2–3 time steps, values start very low (near 0) and stay low or slightly increase; there is no clear initial decrease.  
R2: From about time 3–8, the series remains low and flat, hovering very close to 0.  
R3: From about time 8–13, there is a strong rise from near 0 up to around 0.5–0.6, but this rise starts later (after a longer flat low region) than in class 0.  
R4: After the main peak (around time 14–17), values drop more directly from high to low without a long high plateau.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the very first part of the series (time 0–3), values start clearly above zero (around 0.2–0.3) and then decrease.
R2: During time 3–7, values stay close to the minimum (near 0, forming a short “flat valley”).
R3: The main rise toward the peak begins relatively late, after about time 8.
R4: Before the final decrease, there is only one broad peak region (no clearly separated double-peak).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the very first part of the series (time 0–3), values are very low and almost flat, close to zero.
R2: The main rise toward the peak begins earlier, around time 6–8.
R3: Around the maximum region (roughly time 11–18), the series shows two distinct local peaks separated by a visible dip (a “double-peak” shape).
R4: There is no pronounced early descent from a higher starting value; the increase is mostly from near-zero baseline up to the peaks.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first quarter of the series (roughly indices 0–6), values start moderately high (around 0.2–0.3) and then clearly decrease toward near-zero.
R2: From early-middle to mid-series (roughly indices 7–13), values rise steeply from near-zero up to a single main peak around 0.6–0.7.
R3: In the late-middle region (roughly indices 14–18), values remain relatively high but show only small undulations around 0.5–0.7, without forming a new, higher peak.
R4: In the final part of the series (roughly indices 19–24), values fall steadily from around 0.4–0.5 down to around 0.1–0.2.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first quarter of the series (roughly indices 0–6), values stay very low and flat, close to zero (much lower than in Class 0).
R2: From early-middle to mid-series (roughly indices 7–13), values rise from near-zero to a first peak around 0.5–0.6.
R3: Shortly after this first peak (roughly indices 13–18), there is a second, later peak that is as high as or higher than the first one (around 0.6–0.7), creating a clear “double-peak” shape.
R4: In the final part of the series (roughly indices 19–24), values decline from about 0.4–0.5 down toward about 0.1, similar in slope to Class 0.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first quarter of the series (roughly time 0–6), values start around 0.2–0.3 and clearly decrease toward near 0, rather than staying flat near 0.  
R2: Around time 6–10, values remain very low (close to 0) but then start to rise only after about a third of the series has passed.  
R3: The main peak (maximum value) occurs roughly in the middle of the series (around time 12–15) with height about 0.6–0.7.  
R4: After the peak, values fall again toward about 0.1–0.3 by the end, forming one broad hill without an early flat baseline.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first quarter of the series (time 0–6), values stay close to 0 with only very small fluctuations, forming a flat near‑zero baseline.  
R2: A noticeable rise from near 0 begins later (around time 7–10), after this flat baseline.  
R3: The main peak (maximum value) still occurs around the middle (time 12–17) with height about 0.6–0.7, but the rise from baseline to peak is steeper and starts from closer to 0 than in class 0.  
R4: After the peak, values decrease toward about 0.1–0.2 near the end, again forming a single main hill but preceded by a distinctly flat, near‑zero start segment.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 3–4 time steps, the series starts around 0.2–0.3 and shows a clear downward trend.  
R2: Between time steps 4–8, the series stays very low (close to 0, below roughly 0.08).  
R3: From about time step 8–13, the series rises steeply from near 0 to a peak around 0.6–0.7.  
R4: After the main peak (around time 13), the series gradually decreases toward 0.1–0.2 by the final time steps.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 4–5 time steps, the series stays very low and flat (near 0, below roughly 0.05), with no initial downward trend from a higher value.  
R2: From about time step 5–11, the series rises from near 0 to around 0.5–0.6, but stays below 0.2 until at least time step 7.  
R3: Around time 11–17, the series has its main peak near 0.6–0.7 and may show a minor dip and second rise within this high region.  
R4: After time step 17, the series declines from the peak down to about 0.1–0.2 by the final time steps.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first third of the series (roughly indices 0–8), values start moderately high (>0.15) and then clearly decrease toward near-zero.
R2: Between about indices 8–13, values rise sharply from near-zero up to a single clear peak around 0.6–0.7.
R3: After this main peak, values decrease gradually but stay relatively high (>0.3) for several time steps, forming a broad plateau/hump.
R4: In the last fifth of the series, the values decline clearly from this plateau down to low values (<0.25).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first third of the series (roughly indices 0–8), values remain very low and flat, close to zero (<0.1) without an initial decline from a higher level.
R2: Between about indices 8–13, values rise sharply from this low baseline up to around 0.5–0.6.
R3: After this rise, the series shows a second local peak (a “double top”) where values briefly dip then climb again to another peak near 0.6–0.7.
R4: In the last fifth of the series, values decrease from this second peak down to low values (<0.2).
